answers.field_label: List Of Food And Beverages To Be Sold
answers.value:
Primary menu (all items prepared fresh daily):

Tacos (served on 4" soft corn tortillas, gluten-friendly):
- Pacific Rockfish Baja Taco – Beer-battered local rockfish, shredded cabbage, pico de gallo, chipotle-lime crema, pickled red onions, cilantro, lime wedge.
- Grilled BC Salmon Taco – Grilled wild BC salmon, citrus slaw, avocado crema, roasted corn salsa, fresh dill and cilantro.
- Lions Bay Carnitas Taco – Slow-braised pork shoulder, crispy edges, white onion, cilantro, salsa verde, radish slices.
- Squamish Veggie Taco (Vegan) – Roasted sweet potato, black beans, grilled peppers and onions, corn, vegan cilantro-lime sauce.
- Howe Sound Chicken Tinga Taco – Shredded chicken in a mildly spicy tomato-chipotle sauce, queso fresco, lettuce, crema.

Combos and sides:
- 2-Taco Combo with side of cilantro-lime rice and pinto beans.
- 3-Taco Family Box with tortilla chips and salsa.
- Street Corn Cup (Esquites) – Roasted corn off the cob with cotija cheese, chili-lime mayo, cilantro (can be made without dairy).
- Tortilla Chips with House Salsa Roja.
- Tortilla Chips with Guacamole.

Kids’ options:
- Kids Cheese Quesadilla with side of carrot sticks and ranch.
- Kids Chicken Quesadilla with mild cheese and grilled chicken.

Beverages (non-alcoholic only):
- Bottled still water (500 ml).
- Sparkling water (lime, grapefruit flavors).
- Jarritos Mexican sodas (lime, mandarin, pineapple – rotating flavors).
- House-made Agua Fresca (rotating: watermelon-lime, hibiscus, cucumber-mint) served in 16 oz compostable cups.
- Hot drip coffee (locally roasted beans) and hot tea (herbal and black, individually bagged) during cooler days.

Desserts:
- Churro Bites with cinnamon sugar and chocolate dipping sauce.
- Seasonal Fruit Cup (local berries or stone fruit when available).

Dietary notes:
- Clearly labeled vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-friendly options on the printed and posted menu.
- Can omit dairy, crema, or cheese upon request.
- All allergens (gluten, dairy, fish, shellfish) listed on our menu board and printed takeaway menus.

Pricing:
- Individual tacos: $5–$7 each depending on protein.
- Combos: $14–$19.
- Kids meals: $8–$10.
- Beverages: $2–$5.
- Desserts: $4–$6.

answers.field_label: Location And Description Of Cooking And Or Food Preparation Facilities If Applicable
answers.value:
Food is prepared and cooked primarily inside the licensed food truck unit and partially at our approved commissary kitchen:

On-truck facilities:
- 2-burner propane stove, 24" flat-top griddle, and a small countertop fryer used for fish, churros, and tortilla chips. All cooking equipment is located under a commercial-grade stainless steel exhaust hood with a fire suppression system.
- One upright refrigerator and one under-counter fridge for cold storage of proteins, dairy, and produce during service.
- Separate prep counter with food-grade cutting boards, color-coded for raw meats vs. vegetables to prevent cross-contamination.
- Handwashing sink with hot and cold running water, soap, and single-use paper towels, located near the service window and separated from food prep sinks.
- Separate utensil-washing sink with three compartments (wash, rinse, sanitize) for cleaning utensils and small wares in accordance with Vancouver Coastal Health guidelines.
- All surfaces are stainless steel and are sanitized before opening, during slow periods, and at closing using food-safe sanitizer.

Commissary / off-site kitchen:
- We rent time at West Van Shared Kitchen, 1450 Marine Dr, West Vancouver, BC, which is an approved commissary inspected by Vancouver Coastal Health.
- Bulk prep (marinating meats, cooking large batches of beans and rice, chopping certain vegetables, and making salsas) is completed at the commissary. Food is rapidly cooled, labeled with production and discard dates, and transported to the truck in insulated coolers.
- All transportation of food between the commissary and the truck is done in our dedicated refrigerated van with food-safe bins.

Service layout and customer interaction:
- Service is through a side window facing the sidewalk/park area, with a clearly marked order line and pick-up area to maintain flow and prevent crowding.
- A small external portable hand-sanitizer station is placed near the order window for customers.

Waste and grease management:
- All grease and oil from the fryer are stored in a labeled, sealed container within the truck and disposed of at the commissary’s approved grease disposal facility; nothing is poured into any drains or onto the ground.
- Solid waste (food scraps, wrappers) is kept in covered bins inside the truck and transferred to appropriate garbage/compost/recycling receptacles at the end of each day.

answers.field_label: Any Other Required Approvals Please List I E Fire Inspection Etc
answers.value:
- Vancouver Coastal Health Mobile Food Premises Permit (copy attached).
- Fire Safety Inspection certificate for mobile cooking unit, issued by District of West Vancouver Fire & Rescue Services (current for 2026 season; copy attached).
- Valid BC business license from the District of West Vancouver for mobile food vending (copy attached).
- Commercial vehicle insurance and liability insurance certificate naming the Village of Lions Bay as additional insured (as referenced in the attached agreement form).

answers.field_label: Strategies To Mitigate Impact On Surrounding Land Uses Due To Noise Litter Dust Odor Smoke Or Other Issues And Covid 19 Strategies
answers.value:
Noise control:
- We use a modern, quiet inverter generator mounted on sound-dampening brackets and enclosed in a ventilated acoustic box to minimize operational noise. When shore power is available, we plug into electrical service instead of running the generator.
- No amplified music or loudspeakers will be used. Staff are instructed to speak at normal conversational volume when calling out orders.
- Setup and teardown are done as quietly as possible (no banging of metal trays or dragging equipment) and restricted to the permitted operating hours.

Litter and waste management:
- We provide clearly labeled customer bins for garbage, recycling, and compost (where available) within a few metres of the truck. The bins have lids to prevent litter from blowing away.
- All packaging is either compostable or recyclable where possible (compostable cups, paper boats, wooden forks). We avoid single-use plastics except where food safety absolutely requires it (e.g., sealed water bottles).
- Staff conduct a litter patrol within a 15–20 metre radius of the truck every 30–45 minutes, picking up any stray napkins, cups, or wrappers, whether or not they originated from our truck.
- At the end of each operating day, we do a final sweep of the area to ensure no debris, food scraps, or grease spots are left behind.

Dust, odor, and smoke:
- All cooking involving grease (fish frying, tortilla chips, churros) is done under a properly maintained exhaust hood with filters and fire suppression. Filters are cleaned or replaced regularly to minimize smoke and strong odors.
- We keep frying temperatures at appropriate levels to reduce smoking oil and use high-quality cooking oil that is filtered daily and replaced regularly.
- We position the truck so that the exhaust vents point away from nearby residential properties and park seating areas as much as possible, taking into account prevailing wind direction.
- We do not use open charcoal grills or wood fires, which significantly reduces smoke and particulate matter.

Traffic, crowd, and neighborhood impact:
- We will work within the village’s designated parking and siting requirements to ensure we are not blocking driveways, crosswalks, or emergency access routes.
- We encourage walk-up and bike traffic by offering a small discount for customers who arrive on foot or bicycle when appropriate promotions are allowed.
- Order flow is managed with a simple queue system and clear signage, keeping customers lined up in an orderly fashion and away from the roadway or neighboring business entrances.

COVID-19 and general health safety strategies (also applicable to other respiratory illnesses):
- Staff health screening: All staff are instructed not to report to work if they are experiencing fever, cough, sore throat, or other signs of illness. We maintain a small on-call roster so that sick staff can stay home without pressure.
- Hand hygiene: All staff wash their hands frequently—at minimum on arrival, before putting on gloves, after handling raw foods, after handling cash or payment devices, after using the washroom, after breaks, and any time contamination is suspected. Alcohol-based hand sanitizer is also available at key points inside the truck.
- Masks and PPE: We maintain a supply of disposable masks for staff who choose to wear them during higher-risk periods (e.g., during cold/flu surges) or if required by local guidance. Gloves are worn for direct food handling and changed frequently.
- Contactless payment: We strongly encourage tap payments (debit/credit and mobile wallets) to reduce handling of cash. The payment terminal is wiped down with disinfectant wipes regularly.
- Surface sanitation: High-touch surfaces (order counter, payment terminal, door handles, condiment area) are disinfected at least every hour and more often during busy periods. Food prep surfaces are cleaned and sanitized throughout the day and thoroughly at closing.
- Distancing and line management: We use ground markers or small cones to suggest spacing in the customer queue during busier times, and we maintain a separate pick-up area to reduce clustering at the order window.

Community consideration and communication:
- We post a small, friendly sign with our contact email and phone number so nearby residents or businesses can reach us quickly if there are any concerns about noise, litter, or behavior. We commit to responding promptly and adjusting our operations where reasonable.
- Our staff are trained to be respectful and proactive—for example, reminding groups not to block sidewalks or building entrances and courteously asking customers to dispose of their waste in the provided bins.

Overall, our goal is to operate as a “good neighbor” food truck—keeping the area cleaner than we found it, minimizing noise and odors, and following current public health guidance to protect customers, staff, and the broader Lions Bay community.
